title: interictal iEEG during slow-wave sleep with HFO markings
description: This dataset comprises intracranial electroencephalography (iEEG) recordings from 20 subjects with marked high-frequency oscillations (HFOs) during slow-wave sleep. The data has been converted to BIDS format and includes HFO event annotations (ripples and fast ripples) validated against the original morphology-based detection from the source publication. The dataset serves as a resource for validating open-source HFO detection algorithms and understanding the relationship between HFO resection and seizure outcomes in epilepsy patients.
methods_description: iEEG recordings were obtained during slow-wave sleep. Electrode contacts evoking motor or language responses during electrical stimulation were excluded. In temporal lobe epilepsy patients, only the 3 most mesial bipolar channels were included. HFO events were detected and marked in the original study using morphology-based methods. Data conversion to BIDS format was performed using MNE-BIDS.
